daily_reality.typical_day: 7 meetings on an average day. Daily standup, sprint planning, 1:1s, client demos, cross-team sync. Uses Google Calendar exclusively. Spends 30 min/day scheduling and rescheduling. Team of 8 across 3 timezones (Berlin, Lisbon, Bangalore).
daily_reality.biggest_time_waste: Back-and-forth on meeting times across timezones. Calendly free tier limits to 1 event type. Clients in US timezones need early morning or late evening slots.

psychology.tech_savviness: very high — developer, evaluates tools critically
psychology.risk_tolerance: moderate — willing to try new tools if migration is easy
psychology.decision_speed: fast — tries tool for 5 minutes, decides within a day
trust_signals: Google Calendar sync works on first try
frustration_triggers: can't set buffer time between meetings
ugly/outdated booking page shared with clients

first_screen: Create first event type — guided flow
deal_breakers: Only 1 event type on free tier
expectations.current_pain_level: 6/10 — Calendly works but free tier is too limited, paid is €12/mo for just 1 user
